message.content:
Build the Landing page component for the OmegA web app at /home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/.

This is a Next.js App Router app using CSS Modules (NOT Tailwind). Design tokens:
--void: #02020c; --indigo: #6366f1; --violet: #8b5cf6; --lavender: #c4b5fd; --gold: #e4b84a; --white: #f1f5f9;
Fonts: 'Orbitron' for headers, 'Space Grotesk' for body.

Read the existing page.tsx to understand the current landing/hero section:
/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/app/page.tsx
/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/app/page.module.css
/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/app/globals.css

Extract the landing/hero section into its own component. Create:

1. `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/Landing/Landing.tsx` + `.module.css`
   - Large breathing Omega symbol (Ω) centered — use the existing omega-breathe animation from globals.css
   - "OmegA" title below in Orbitron, large and bold
   - Subtitle: "Sovereign. Persistent. Self-Knowing." in Space Grotesk
   - Feature cards (3-4): brief descriptions of what OmegA can do (e.g. "Persistent Memory", "Identity Anchoring", "Synthesis Engine", "Raw Data Access")
   - Cards should have glass background, subtle hover glow, staggered fade-up entrance
   - Input field at the bottom — same style as the main InputArea but centered
   - "Begin a conversation" placeholder
   - When user submits from landing, it should call onFirstMessage(text)

Props:
- onFirstMessage: (text: string) => void

2. Keep the existing OmegaCanvas particle background concept. If there's a canvas element in the current page.tsx, preserve that logic as a `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/OmegaCanvas/OmegaCanvas.tsx` + `.module.css` component. It should be a full-screen fixed canvas at z-index 0 behind everything, with pointer-events: none.

Make the landing page breathtaking — this is the first impression. The Omega should feel alive. The cards should feel like floating glass panels in space. Animations should be smooth and purposeful.

content.text: I'll start by reading the existing files to understand the current structure.

content.text:
Good. OmegaCanvas already exists as a component and is already used properly (fixed, z-index 0, pointer-events none). Now I'll create the Landing component and its CSS module, then refactor the OmegaCanvas into its own directory.

Let me create the files.
Now move OmegaCanvas into its own directory with an index file, and create the index for Landing too.

input.content:
export { default } from "./OmegaCanvas";
export type { CanvasState } from "./OmegaCanvas";

content.text: Now update page.tsx to use the new Landing component and check what imports reference the old OmegaCanvas path.

The old `@/components/OmegaCanvas` import in page.tsx will now resolve to the `index.ts` in the new directory. The old single-file `OmegaCanvas.tsx` still exists -- let me check if the dynamic import also resolves correctly, then clean up.

The dynamic import on line 11 does `import("@/components/OmegaCanvas")` which should resolve to the new `OmegaCanvas/index.ts`. The old file at `/web/src/components/OmegaCanvas.tsx` would conflict with the directory. I need to remove it.
Now update page.tsx to use the Landing component, replacing the inline landing section.

content.content: The file /home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/app/page.tsx has been updated successfully.

content.text: Let me verify it builds (or at least check for obvious issues).

Clean compile. Here is what was done:

**Created files:**

1. `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/Landing/Landing.tsx` -- Self-contained landing page component with:
   - Breathing Omega glyph (uses `omega-breathe` / `omega-think` animations from globals.css)
   - "OmegA" title in Orbitron with "Sovereign. Persistent. Self-Knowing." subtitle
   - 4 glass feature cards (Persistent Memory / MYELIN, Identity Anchoring / AEON, Synthesis Engine / ADCCL, Governed Reasoning / AEGIS) with staggered fade-up entrance, glass sheen sweep on hover, and glow lift
   - Centered input field with "Begin a conversation" placeholder
   - `onFirstMessage(text)` prop fires on submit; `isThinking` prop controls thinking-state visuals
   - Greek letter icons per card for architectural layer identity

2. `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/Landing/Landing.module.css` -- All styles: 4-column responsive grid (2-col at 768px, 1-col at 420px), card glass backgrounds with `backdrop-filter: blur(16px)`, hover glow/lift, staggered `card-enter` keyframes, ring-pulse for thinking state

3. `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/Landing/index.ts` -- Barrel export

4. `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/OmegaCanvas/OmegaCanvas.tsx` -- Moved from the old single-file location, unchanged logic (full-screen fixed canvas, z-index 0, pointer-events none, particle field with connections and field lines)

5. `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/OmegaCanvas/index.ts` -- Barrel export with `CanvasState` type re-export

**Modified:**
- `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/app/page.tsx` -- Replaced the inline ~90-line landing section with `<Landing onFirstMessage={...} isThinking={...} />`. The `onFirstMessage` callback directly invokes the chat stream logic so the transition to chat mode is seamless.

**Removed:**
- `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/OmegaCanvas.tsx` -- Superseded by directory version

TypeScript compiles cleanly with no errors.
